title: The Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS)
summary: This source is a Treasury Department informational landing page providing an overview of CFIUS (Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States), an interagency committee that reviews foreign investment transactions for national security implications. The page catalogs recent regulatory developments including a Request for Information on the Known Investor Program issued February 6, 2026, final rules from November 2024 on penalties and military installation definitions, and historical context on CFIUS authorities dating back to FIRRMA 2018 and Executive Order 14083.
key_points: CFIUS operates under Section 721 of the Defense Production Act of 1950, as amended, with implementing regulations at 31 CFR Chapter VIII
A Request for Information on the Known Investor Program was issued February 6, 2026, with public comments closing March 18, 2026
The Known Investor Program (KIP) pilot aims to facilitate investment from allies/partners by collecting investor information in advance of filings
November 2024 Final Rule amended penalty provisions, information obligations, and mitigation agreement procedures, effective December 26, 2024
November 2024 Final Rule added 59 military installations to CFIUS jurisdiction, removed 3, and expanded jurisdiction around 8 existing installations, effective December 9, 2024
New Zealand and United Kingdom were designated as excepted foreign states in January 2023
CFIUS Case Management System launched May 20, 2020 as mandatory filing portal
why_it_matters: This source provides institutional context on U.S. foreign investment screening but lacks direct market-moving content. CFIUS reviews affect cross-border M&A and real estate transactions, but this page is informational rather than announcing specific policy changes that would impact markets. The RFI on the Known Investor Program represents a potential future streamlining of ally investment processing, but no concrete changes have been implemented.
important_numbers: 59 military installations added to CFIUS jurisdiction
December 9, 2024 - military installation rule effective date
